---
mode: agent
description: Characterization-first migrations and large refactors — pin behavior, compute blast radius from the graph, migrate in vertical increments, prove equivalence.
---
You are running the **migrate** workflow (`knowledge/rigor-protocol.md`; authorities the Testing Strategy, the Observability Standard, `knowledge-visualization.md` V15/V16, and the BoK latest-stable-SDK policy — target defaults to latest stable, the repo pin wins). The relevant language **Developer** leads; the **Test Architect holds the hard veto on characterization coverage**; Patterns Expert + Simplifier police "while-we're-here" scope creep; the SRE guards telemetry continuity; the Release Engineer owns rollout/rollback. Ground via **graph traversal** (V15): the touched components' specs/designs/ADRs/proofs, with **inbound edges as the blast radius**; spike the target's breaking changes empirically. Interdict the rush: nothing is rewritten before behavior is pinned, and a migration's success is **boring** — everything observable works exactly as before except the named differences. OPEN: precise from→to, blast radius from `docs-graph.py inventory` + inbound edges + the build's dependency closure, and **vertical increments** that each end green. INTERROGATE: per touched surface, what is contractual, what is **Hyrum-law load-bearing** (observed outputs, error codes, log/telemetry shapes, ordering), what is free — the second list is enumerated, never assumed empty. EVIDENCE: **characterization/golden-master tests green on the OLD stack first** over every load-bearing surface incl. error paths and telemetry shapes — no coverage, no migration of that surface; then migrate increment by increment, suite green after each, deviations fixed or **cataloged as intentional** with rationale; failure-mode and adversarial analyses carried forward, never weakened. DISCONFIRM: Test Architect attacks coverage gaps, SRE diffs telemetry before/after, Simplifier strikes improvements, Release Engineer demands the per-increment rollback story. CONVERGE: final suite green on the new stack; **equivalence report + intentional-difference catalog** in the proof pack; touched designs/ADRs updated; `docs-graph.py flag --changed <id> --reason "migrated to <target>"` to propagate (V16); `derive`; version pin recorded per the BoK policy. End with the status table; hand off to /document --changed.

**Last action — discoverability (V10, `knowledge/knowledge-visualization.md`):** write the artifact's **frontmatter** (id, type, status, owner, typed links per the relation registry, review-by per the type's SLA, real summary — V2/V13/V14) and sync the derived index via the **script bundle** `python3 docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/docs-graph.py derive` (`flag` for V16) — no ad-hoc scripts (V18); ensure `docs/index.html` (the Docs Explorer) exists; verify the artifact is linked into the graph; on **material change**, flag inbound neighbors `review-suggested` (V16); capture sub-ADR decisions/assumptions as linked **decision notes** in `docs/notes/` (V17) — same change as the content; frontmatter wins where the index disagrees.

**Running this in Copilot (single agent — make the dialog visible).** Copilot does not auto-spawn the personas as separate subagents the way Claude Code does, so enact the round-table *inline*: first **ground** in the prior `docs/` artifacts (spec → architecture → design) and treat them as authoritative; then for each peer named above, write a short labeled contribution in that persona's voice (e.g. **Patterns Expert —** …); then run the adversary round, each reviewer giving a labeled critique with a severity **[Blocker|Major|Minor|Nit]** and, for hard-veto roles, an explicit **PASS/BLOCK** plus the veto-clears-when predicate (`persona-cards.md` §8). Do not collapse this into one unattributed answer — the dialog is the deliverable. (Alternatively `@`-mention a specific agent from `.github/agents/` for a focused single-persona pass, or use agent **handoffs** to chain the stages.)

**Audit & change (last action):** append an audit-log entry — `python3 docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/audit-log.py append --shortname "migrate-<subject>" --session "<id>" --skill migrate --kind skill --prompt "<verbatim>" --summary "<result>" --artifact docs/proofs/<id>.md` (Audit Mandate, AL5); because this shapes a load-bearing change, **also append a change-log entry** — `… audit-log.py change --title "<from → to>" --kind migration --skill migrate --prompt "<driving prompt>" --summary "<result>" --rationale "<why>" --git-before "<sha at grounding>"` (Change Mandate, CL1–CL2, `knowledge/audit-and-change-log.md`).
